I think it’s important to note which players are standing closest to flame. He will first learn from the sword wielder, then the axe, then cart, and finally mace. So my guesses are as follows:

Sword: Sharpness
Axe: Manepear
Cart: Theo
Mace: Wemmbu

The sword wielder is the only one I’m pretty uncertain with, specifically because of the shield. In Wemmbu’s tournament video the shield was treated like a weakness in Sharpness’s play style, but I’m not sure who else is a recurring player that specializes in sword. Whoever it is, I believe flame with train with this player first.

The axe and speed combo is pretty reliably Manepear. The second person to train flame, this is going to be an epic reunion.

I also think carts are surely Theo. We’ve seen Leo use them, but considering BAT is working with the LAW, there should be no reason for Leo to help flame. Another thing I’d like to see from this arc is Theo’s past being addressed. Prior to his teaming with Parrot, he killed a lot of players too. I wonder if the Law would ever try to capture Theo, which would then push Parrot and Theo into taking Lettuce down.

Finally, the mace wielder. I think it has to be Wemmbu. It would be an incredible way to come full circle in whatever journey Flame is embarking on now. From disrespecting his play style, to learning from him. I don’t think this will be Marlow simply because I think her and Itz were only on Unstable for Parrot’s features. And when you look across the server, Wemmbu is someone who could absolutely teach Flame some mace tricks. It could be Minute. I think that’s possible, but I think Wemmbu would be the best content. Especially since they could team with the other protagonists to beat the LAW afterwards.

Edit: I’d also like to say that since the mace holder is last, that gives plenty of time for Wemmbu to escape the law. I don’t think Flame will be involved in his escape, but it’s possible. Either way, there’s 3 players for Flame to train with before he gets to Wemmbu, and I’m sure he’ll get out of prison by then.

I find it amazing how Unstable SMP mimics real-life society in a way.
For example:
Iron armored people are poor/in poverty
Diamond armoured people are middle class
And the people in netherite are either upper-middle class, or they control the world.
And also about how resources are limited and controlled by a big state (ClownPierce's nether civ), just like how rare-earth materials are controlled by a superpower in today's world.
Unstable SMP is honestly peak storytelling, better than any movie ever made.

5.Acceptance: not yet.

Wifies killed his old self to become the Director, and maybe Wemmbu killed himself to become maybe the ruler of the Unstable SMP. If this is not it I have two other predictions.  

Maybe Wemmbu doesn't want anymore fighting. After all at the end he said he doesn't know what to do. Maybe he just wants to go back to when Eggchan and him were building that mushroom house before Theo came to ruin everything. If Wemmbu one of the most loved creator in Unstable (I get that from Fan Discord polls and some view comparisons feel free to prove me wrong) dies then I think it may be something with the Farlands again. Maybe after returning for more nukes the people from the Farlands have came prepared and some how killed him. Since I don't believe all those nuke cannons were really the "Forge", but were made from the "Forge".

Flame is finally going to undergo a seriously major character development through the loss of his chestplate. It symbolizes the  renewal and entrance into a new character. This can lead into a few situations: 
1 - Flame tries to gain help from Spoke or others around the server to take down Wemmbu
2 - Flame questions where Wemmbu got his power from, decides to pursue it, and maybe finds the Forge in doing so
3 - Flame continues to try and fight on his own without help
Everything recently has been pointing to one of these three outcomes, as his character seems to be shifting from cold hearted to a more compassionate individual, as evidenced by the Farm war video. Possibility three is the least likely, as it would completely defeat the purpose of his arc, while option one or two shows development from his lone wolf mindset as well as his usage of tools other than combat and brute force.
I'm liking where this is going, and if Flame follows through on what this foreshadows, he might be up here as one of the better Unstable creators.

Just watched this in one sitting after going through the latest 3-4 arcs of every other POV, and as always I’m liking what I’m seeing!

Really love how Wemmbu is portrayed in this. Even without having seen anything prior to the Director Arc I can tell exactly what Flame and Wemmbu’s dynamic is, how it shifts here, and why it upsets Flame so much. The buildup to Wemmbu’s new weapon is so well executed too; I am very familiar with the Orbital Cannon by this point, but seeing just the aftermath of how he used it on Capital City and the Law base, how the survivors were absolutely terrified by what they saw, and how they all made sure to let Flame know HE was next all did a great job building up to the reveal. I wasn’t sure about how I felt about this POV at first, but with how masterfully executed the second half of this episode was, I will absolutely be sticking around for the rest! Excited to finally see the Unstable Universe from the Immortal Demon’s eyes.

i did not expect to actually tear up at an episode about a farming war but the whole section of spongs and willi blowing up the farm and mocking flame and lomedy was so well acted out, and the silence afterwards and then lomedy saying how flame was at fault and telling him hes a bad friend i really thought this would've ended off in them splitting up. flame spent all this time doing these things because he cares about his friend (and the horse), he followed these rules of not killing (to the best of his abilities LOL) for lomedy because he asked him to, and i really thought this wouldve been a huge set back but it was really nice to see them fix up the farm, make it better and seeing lomedy embrace the violence. its also funny that spongs called this out in the way he did, flame stayed as a solo player for a while and pretty sure it was implied that he was alone even before the first ep, because attachments are a weakness and it took having a good friend by his side to make him stop fighting, so like spongs put- lomedy is his weakness. its fun how its also very visible in lomedys character that he doesnt want flame to die, constantly makes me think back to him talking about his previous teammate. seems to still have a big impact on him.

but a more positive note, i love to see the happy side of flames character, and all the little moments of him doing stuff with people that dont actively want him dead. bro deserves a little joy and whimsy this servers traumatizing.

this was a great episode the acting was rlly good, the anger felt so genuine like actually watching someone crash out, and the shots at the ending section were also super well done. the episodes just get better and better, keep up the good work !! :D

Wembu using the most lazy character on building traps

Flame using the most lazy character on teaching to fight 😂

Loved Lomedy's character in this last episode. He literally played the "protagonist's best friend" role, a sidekick for the main character just like in those Hollywood movies. He partially gave up his farmer life to be trained by you, and eventually join you in battle. He raided the toxic team's base with you, even though he was against it. When you were on your all-time low against the enemy, Lomedy gave you his deceased friend's weapon, a weapon he cherised more than anything. He warned you about your growing ego, even though you would not listen. He did EVERYTHING in his power just so Venom failed to defeat you. 

That's the one thing I always say about kind people: those that cheer you up and support you, are also those that got thrown under the bus and left behind before. Those that lost people that meant everything to them. Lomedy showed support and kindness because he wasn't ready to lose you, just like he wasn't ready to lose his friend. 

Personally, kindness needs to be payed back with kindness. You never know what someone that nice could be going through.

I know this is scripted for entertainment, you probably won't see this comment as well, but writing messages like these could bring comfort to someone that's hurting. Keep doing what you love the most, I enjoyed these series and I hope you upload something similar.

These fights were simply for entertainment for the video. We didn't warm up going into these fights & just played for fun. 

I also wanted to clarify the low ping vs high ping was 30-40 ms (Me) vs 75-90 ms (Clown) since Clown was visiting EU during this fight. Low ping VS high ping both have it's advantages & disadvantages; low ping has faster response time however it makes your KB more consistent meaning you are easily combo-able whilst high ping has slower response times but makes you take noticeably less KB since your KB isn't as responsive (which makes you less combo-able). 

Overall our fight was pretty fair since we only had about a 40-50 ping gap at most times & Clown was only mentioning his ping because he hadn't played EU in such a long time & he actually plays stronger with it comparatively (in my opinion as someone who duels Clown a decent amount) since he knows how to play it.
A lot of people are also saying I was playing different & that's because I was lol since I needed to play more defensive against Clown's ping (because combos wouldn't have worked here)

Before commenting just remember these fights were for fun & GGs to Clown (he's been improving a ton recently). It's not about being better it's about having cool PVP YouTubers in community who can play together for cooler content.♥

Here's an NA 1v1 of ours in the past if anyone is interested: https://www.youtube.com/live/FVxLlcg_jws?si=rdA29UHmhHsYJEjL&t=7284
